summary.intent: Run local validation checks and fix any failures before committing and pushing changes.
summary.outcome: A biome formatting violation in providers.js was fixed by manually editing the file and committed. The fix broke a long console.warn() call into multiple lines to satisfy the formatter.

repo: local-validate.sh runs parallel checks: install-names, i18n, biome, zizmor, fmt — all must pass
Biome is used for JS formatting; running with --write may not persist changes in all environments
The script detects macOS without nvcc and forces non-CUDA validation commands automatically

code.finding: Biome requires long console.warn() calls to be split across multiple lines rather than kept on a single line

workflow: biome --write may not always persist file changes; falling back to manual Edit is necessary
Verifying git diff after automated fixes is essential before assuming changes were saved

friction: biome --write appeared to succeed but did not persist changes to disk, requiring manual file editing
The assistant reported the fix was applied before verifying it was actually saved, causing confusion on commit
